situational_context: Running comeback week 7: 10 structured runs since May 3 (44 days in). Run #10 on June 14 consolidated at 4.9 km Z2 (HR 136, suffer 18) — well controlled. ACWR easing from 1.35 to 1.32, still marginally above 1.3 threshold but trending down. HRV 7d avg recovering to 36 ms (overnight 38 ms BALANCED) — fatigue signal from early June fading. Weight stable at 102.6 kg. E-bike commuting continues as primary modality on sur-site days (today: 39 km round trip, suffer 15). HEATWAVE dominates the week: 32-38C through June 22, peaking 38C Sunday June 21. All outdoor training must be heat-adapted with reduced HR ceilings and mandatory hydration. VO2max running steady at 40.6. Vitamin D retest still due ~July 2026. Baby-related sleep disruptions remain a background factor; activity compliance may be partial — this is expected and not a concern.

plan_7d_ahead.type: Sortie velo — 60 min ebike Z1-Z2 (canicule extreme 38C)

recommendations.text: HEATWAVE PROTOCOL (32-38C all week): Cap all outdoor sessions at Z2 strict. Reduce running HR ceiling by 7-10 bpm from normal (max 128 bpm for runs). Carry 500+ mL water for runs, 750+ mL for bike rides. Abort immediately if lightheaded, nauseous, or HR won't settle below 140. Hydrate proactively — aim 3L+ daily. Prefer shaded routes (Bois de Vincennes).
recommendations.measurable: Zero heat-related symptoms this week; all session avg HR below 135 bpm

recommendations.text: ACWR is 1.32 (still marginally above 1.3). Continue conservative load management — the natural decline from running consolidation at 4 km and rest days should bring ACWR to 1.25 or below by week end. Do not add volume beyond planned sessions.
recommendations.measurable: ACWR at or below 1.25 by June 22

recommendations.text: HRV recovering (35 to 36, overnight 38 BALANCED). Maintain 7+ hours sleep despite heatwave. Cool bedroom to 22C or below, use fan, consider cold shower before bed. Heatwave-disrupted sleep could reverse HRV recovery — prioritize sleep hygiene this week.
recommendations.measurable: HRV 7d avg at or above 37 ms by June 22; sleep 7h+ per night all week

markers_to_watch.threshold: Alert if ACWR rises back above 1.35 or fails to drop below 1.3 by June 22
markers_to_watch.reason: Currently 1.32, trending down but still above safe zone

markers_to_watch.threshold: Alert if HRV drops below 33 ms or fails to reach 37 ms by June 22
markers_to_watch.reason: Recovering from mid-35 range; heatwave stress could reverse gains

markers_to_watch.threshold: Any nausea, excessive HR, headache during outdoor sessions triggers abort and 48h rest
markers_to_watch.reason: Heatwave 32-38C with 102 kg body weight = high thermal stress during running
